codeReview.summary: The fix directly addresses the prior blocking issue. `compute_quality_summary()` now detects low recognition coverage for legacy CSV problem IDs and sets `weighted_pct` to NaN so `_format_quality_cell()` falls back to flat `quality_pct` as primary. Added regression coverage validates all-unknown, mostly-unknown, rendered-cell fallback, and recognized-problem non-fallback behavior.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Architecture docs remain out of sync with current scoring implementation details.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/architecture.md:146-153 documents category-weighted `CATEGORY_WEIGHTS`, while actual code uses tier-weighted `TIER_WEIGHTS` and problem-tier mapping in mtb/quality_benchmarks/scoring.py:22-31,61-83.

summary: Reviewed fix feature commit 3cc683f against the original failure in readme-table-weighted-scoring (commit 40e03a7). The implemented fallback logic and new regression tests adequately resolve the blocking legacy-CSV quality-cell issue; review passes.
